description: Add Playwright E2E specs for core user journeys - expand the existing e2e/tests/ directory with specs for calendar, meet, and drive flows. Also expand the accessibility spec to cover more routes across the ecosystem apps.
steps: Create e2e/tests/calendar/create-event.spec.ts with a Playwright test that navigates to the calendar app, opens the create event form, fills it out, submits, and verifies the event appears on the calendar grid. Use the auth-helpers fixture pattern from e2e/fixtures/auth-helpers.ts.

Create e2e/tests/meet/join-meeting.spec.ts with a Playwright test that navigates to quantmeet, creates a room, lands on the pre-join lobby, and verifies the control bar is rendered with expected buttons (mic, cam, share, leave).
Create e2e/tests/drive/upload-file.spec.ts with a Playwright test that navigates to quantdrive, triggers a file upload via the UI, and verifies the file appears in the file list.
Extend e2e/tests/a11y/a11y.spec.ts to add more routes: '/calendar', '/meet', '/drive', '/sync', '/tube', '/neon', '/docs' to the ROUTES_TO_TEST array to broaden accessibility coverage.
acceptance_criteria: e2e/tests/calendar/create-event.spec.ts exists and is syntactically valid TypeScript
e2e/tests/meet/join-meeting.spec.ts exists and is syntactically valid TypeScript
e2e/tests/drive/upload-file.spec.ts exists and is syntactically valid TypeScript
e2e/tests/a11y/a11y.spec.ts includes at least 10 routes in ROUTES_TO_TEST
pnpm --filter quant-e2e exec tsc --noEmit passes (E2E TypeScript is valid)
findings: e2e directory is not part of pnpm workspace (not in pnpm-workspace.yaml). Used npm install --no-package-lock to install e2e/node_modules for tsc verification. Prettier ran via lint-staged on commit. All 4 spec files compile cleanly. a11y ROUTES_TO_TEST expanded from 4 to 11 entries.
